SECNOTE_UI_03: The message is encrypted in your browser with AES-256-GCM (WebCrypto). The server receives only ciphertext, and the key stays after # in the URL and is never sent to the server.
SECNOTE_UI_05: The note is atomically deleted after reading.
SECNOTE_UI_09: API protection against spam and brute force.
SECNOTE_UI_11: Create a link and send it only to a trusted recipient.
SECNOTE_UI_21: The key exists only after #. If you lose the link, it cannot be recovered.
SECNOTE_UI_23: Read mode is available only through the full link with parameters.
SECNOTE_UI_24: The read field is available only through a link in the format ?p=<nid>#<key>. The request will be sent to the server only after confirmation.
SECNOTE_UI_25: Are you sure you want to read the note? After opening it will be deleted forever.
SECNOTE_UI_29: The server stores only nid, blob, and timestamps.
SECNOTE_UI_30: Anti-spam uses a client hash in the form SHA-256(IP + server salt).
SECNOTE_UI_31: The note is deleted after the first read, by TTL, or after a service restart.
SECNOTE_UI_32: Whoever has the full link can read the note.
SECNOTE_UI_34: You enter text and choose TTL.
SECNOTE_UI_35: The browser encrypts the text locally; the key is not sent to the server.
SECNOTE_UI_36: The service issues a short nid and stores only ciphertext.
SECNOTE_UI_37: The recipient opens the link once and gets the decrypted text.
SECNOTE_UI_38: Important: this service protects data on the server, but does not protect against an infected device or a tampered frontend.
SECNOTE_UI_39: If needed, specify another API via ?api=https://host and use a local frontend copy with the same parameter.
SECNOTE_UI_42: By creating a note you agree to our
HOW_SIMPLE_1_D: Any text — a password, a link, a secret note.
HOW_SIMPLE_2_D: Your browser encrypts it and generates a unique one-time URL.
HOW_SIMPLE_3_D: Send via any channel — only the link holder can read it.
HOW_SIMPLE_4_D: The message is permanently deleted after the first read.
HOW_TECH_1: AES-256-GCM browser-side encryption via WebCrypto API — key never leaves your device.
HOW_TECH_2: Key lives in URL #fragment only — not sent to server, not in logs.
HOW_TECH_3: Server stores only ciphertext + note ID in RAM — no disk writes.
HOW_TECH_4: Proof-of-Work (SHA-256) protects the API against spam and brute-force.
HOW_TECH_5: Note deleted atomically on first read or when TTL expires.
SECNOTE_LNG_ERR_01: Select API by ?api=https://host or meta api-base-url
SECNOTE_LNG_ERR_02: api-base-url must start with http:// or https://
SECNOTE_LNG_MSG_04: Note created. Send the full link only to a trusted recipient.
SECNOTE_LNG_MSG_07: Note decrypted ({provider}). It has already been deleted on the server.
HOW_TECH_BITS: the secret or "password" part after # is a random 256-bit key. the shared URL also carries a separate 128-bit note ID in ?p=. together the full URL contains 384 bits of identifiers, but only the 256-bit fragment is confidential and required for decryption.
HOW_TECH_SELFHOST: the frontend is a single static HTML file. if you host it on the same domain as the backend, it uses that API automatically. use ?api=https://your-host only for a local copy or a separate frontend host.
HOW_RUN_LOCAL_HINT: download the frontend zip and open it in any browser. point it at your own server with ?api=https://your-host. the encryption key never leaves your device.
COMPOSE_OPEN_HINT: paste a full shared URL here to open it with this copy, even if you run the frontend locally.
LINK_SUB_PREFIX: copy and send it. opens once. expires in
LINK_KEY_HINT: ↑ everything after # is the key — never sent to server
GATE_WARN_TITLE: reading this note will permanently destroy it.
GATE_WARN_SUB: it can only be read once. as soon as you open it, the server deletes it immediately and it cannot be recovered.
READ_DESTROYED_HINT: this note has been destroyed on the server. it lives only on this page until you close it.
BURNED_SUB: this note was already read and destroyed. each note can only be opened once — after that it's gone from the server forever.
BURNED_SELF_DESTRUCT: notes self-destruct immediately after the first read
SETTINGS_API_HINT: where notes are stored. use your own self-hosted instance, or the public one.
SETTINGS_OFFLINE_HINT: this page works fully offline — open the .html locally and point it at any compatible server.
